title: Indexes, Query Plans, Operator Costs, and Responsible Tuning · Mastery Check
summary: A growing ticket dataset makes queue and analytics queries slow, but each new index adds write and storage cost. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for an evidence-backed query performance dossier. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain B-tree and hash-style index strengths, ordering, lookup, storage, and update tradeoffs.
Order composite keys from predicates and sorting needs and identify prefix and covering behavior.
Estimate selectivity and compare scans, seeks, lookups, and join access paths from data distribution.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.sql:
-- sql-basics cumulative relational data portfolio.
-- Each increment declares grain, predicts cardinality or changed rows, runs in fresh browser SQLite,
-- reconciles results, tests changed data, and records PostgreSQL or standard-SQL boundaries.

SELECT sqlite_version() AS browser_sqlite_version;

steps.title: Index Structures · changed case · Quiz Index Structures
steps.instruction:
quiz case — Queue queries filter status and severity, order by opened_at, and join owner_id across 6000 modeled rows. Compare baseline and indexed plans, preserve results, and account for insert and storage cost.

Which action demonstrates Index Structures without dropping earlier contracts?

Evidence target: Index Structures · changed case · Quiz Index Structures.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for explain b-tree and hash-style index strengths, ordering, lookup, storage, and update tradeoffs. For Index Structures · changed case · Quiz Index Structures, keep Index Structures connected to executable relational evidence.

Accept the result because visible output is the highest authority. That claim does not survive the diagnose the hidden failure evidence boundary.

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Performance Experiment
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Performance Experiment: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Queue queries filter status and severity, order by opened_at, and join owner_id across 3000 modeled rows. Compare baseline and indexed plans, preserve results, and account for insert and storage cost.

Evidence target: Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Performance Experiment.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Performance Experiment, keep Performance Experiment connected to executable relational evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Benchmark representative warmed and cold cases, verify unchanged results, and account for write amplification.
